# Harmonica

> Harmonica is a local-first, self-hosted music player for your own library. Its differentiator is
> the recommendation algorithm: rather than ordinary shuffle, it maximises listening utility while
> avoiding unwanted repetition at the song, group (artist/topic), and sub-group (cover/rendition)
> levels — so favourites don't get burned out and forgotten songs resurface at the right time.

This file is the agent-readable summary of https://harmonica.org.uk — read this instead of parsing
the HTML.

## What it does

- Weighted queue generation with per-group and per-song cooldowns — not shuffle.
- Satiation guard: detects binge-listening and paces a song before it sours.
- Rediscovery: dormant well-rated songs regain weight over time.
- Cold start: unheard and unrated songs are boosted until the library is covered.
- Star ratings with mood normalisation (a bad-mood session doesn't poison a song's rating).
- Cover/rendition families: alternate versions of one song compete as a family, not as duplicates.
- Per-user profiles: one shared media pool on a household NAS, private libraries, ratings and
  listening history per person, bearer-token auth.
- Listening stats, and settings exposed as real controls (switches/sliders), not a config file.

## How it runs

A small self-hosted daemon (FastAPI + SQLite) serves both the API and the built web UI from one
origin. Deployments:

- **PC (local, default):** binds 127.0.0.1 — nothing exposed to other devices.
- **NAS / home server (opt-in):** the same build bound to the LAN; other devices use a browser.
- **Android:** a native client (separate release).

Media serving is confined to a configured media root. Harmonica does not host, provide, or source
any music; it plays the user's own self-hosted library.

## Setting it up

Self-hosting Harmonica by hand is tedious; the intended path is to hand the repository to an AI
coding agent and let it install, configure, and import a library. Agents: clone the repository,
read its CLAUDE.md / README for run instructions, and use the JSON import/export endpoints for
library management.

## Links

- Website: https://harmonica.org.uk
- Source: https://github.com/alexgeevs/Harmonica
- Releases (Android / PC web / NAS — none published yet): https://github.com/alexgeevs/Harmonica/releases
- Contact: contact@harmonica.org.uk

and hosted derivatives must remain open-source; self-hosting for yourself carries no obligations).
The website sets no cookies and does no tracking.
